metadata.role_title: Applied ML Engineer - GenAI (Post Training) / RAG and AgenticAI Frameworks
metadata.funding_stage: Series B ($17.7M total -- Aavishkaar Capital, Info Edge Ventures, Samsung Venture Investment)
metadata.recent_signal: Feb 2026: Inya VoiceOS (5B param voice-to-voice model) inaugurated by PM Modi at India AI Impact Summit. March 2026: $10M Series B. May 2026: 8 senior leadership hires.
metadata.match_reason: Two active JDs: Post Training (SFT/DPO/LoRA/QLoRA/HuggingFace TRL, Indic LLMs) and RAG/Agentic (LangGraph, Milvus, Qdrant, vLLM). Both map directly to Amit's HuggingFace + LangGraph + Milvus + production RAG stack.
metadata.cold_outreach_angle: Inya VoiceOS launch (PM Modi, India AI Impact Summit Feb 2026) is a live, specific hook -- Amit's production RAG (LangGraph, Milvus, 50K+ q/month) and HuggingFace audio work (IIT Delhi research, WeepScope CNN+GRU) connect to both the Post Training and RAG/Agentic JDs.
metadata.notes: 169+ employees, >50-person rules apply (no founders/CEO/CTO). Email: firstname.lastname@gnani.ai. JD 1: Post Training (SFT, DPO, LoRA/QLoRA, HuggingFace TRL). JD 2: RAG/Agentic (LangGraph, Milvus, vLLM). Selected by IndiaAI Mission.

research_summary: Gnani.ai builds India's voice-first agentic AI platform. Feb 2026: launched Inya VoiceOS (5B param voice-to-voice model, PM Modi inauguration) and Vachana STT/TTS under IndiaAI Mission. $10M Series B closed March 2026. 169+ employees, 30M+ daily voice interactions, 200+ enterprise clients. Two active JDs: Post Training (SFT/DPO/LoRA/HuggingFace TRL) and RAG/Agentic (LangGraph/Milvus/vLLM) -- both match Amit's exact production stack. Email pattern: firstname.lastname@gnani.ai.
jd_full_text:
=== ROLE 1: Applied ML Engineer - GenAI (Post Training) ===
URL: https://careers.gnani.ai/apply/App-Ben-2026-01-21-24
Exp: 3-5 years | Bengaluru Onsite

As part of the IndiaAI Mission, lead post-training, alignment, and instruction fine-tuning of India's foundational LLMs.

Key Responsibilities:
- SFT pipelines for instruction-following and chat models; multi-turn chat datasets (roleplay, troubleshooting, reasoning, long-context)
- Alignment: DPO, GRPO, RFT, reward modeling
- Drive fine-tuning for low-resource Indic languages (Bodo, Manipuri, Santali, Konkani, Dogri)
- Dataset versioning, automated validation, deduplication, toxicity filtering, heuristic checks
- Fine-tuning libraries: HuggingFace TRL, OpenRLHF, DeepSpeed-Chat, NVIDIA NeMo Alignment, Axolotl, Colossal-AI
- LoRA/QLoRA/PEFT workflows at scale

Required: Python, PyTorch, HuggingFace ecosystem, LLM post-training experience. Indic language alignment preferred.

=== ROLE 2: Applied ML Engineer - GenAI (RAG and AgenticAI Frameworks) ===
URL: https://careers.gnani.ai/apply/App-Sal-2026-01-21-27
Exp: 4-8+ years

Key Responsibilities:
- End-to-end RAG: ingestion -> chunking -> embeddings -> indexing -> retrieval -> reranking -> grounded generation
- Hybrid search, multi-hop retrieval, query rewriting, multilingual Indic language support
- Vector DBs: Qdrant, MongoDB Atlas Vector Search, FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus/Zilliz, Elastic/OpenSearch
- Multi-agent workflows: LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, LangChain LCEL; ReAct, Reflexion, Tree-of-Thought
- LLM inference: vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, TGI with quantization and model routing
- Document intelligence: OCR with Unstructured, DocTR, LayoutLMv3
- Evaluation: Ragas, DeepEval, LangSmith
- Nice-to-have: knowledge graph integration, long-context models (128k-200k tokens), open-source contributions

Required: Qdrant + MongoDB Vector Search expertise, open-source LLMs (LLaMA, Qwen, Mistral), 4-8+ yrs ML engineering.
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to mirror both JDs: agentic RAG pipelines (LangGraph + Milvus, 95% MRR, 50K+ q/month), vLLM inference optimization, and audio/speech ML research (CNN-BiLSTM, GRU, IIT Delhi, Samsung Top 10) -- all JD-explicit signals. Technical Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques moved to first with SFT/LoRA/QLoRA/LLM Fine-tuning added (post-training JD explicitly requires these). Vector DBs moved to second with Qdrant listed first (RAG JD marks Qdrant as 'Essential'). Languages section updated to show Hugging Face TRL (post-training JD tool). Infrastructure updated: vLLM and DocTR made visible (both RAG JD requirements). Hardware/IoT row dropped -- pure ML/voice AI roles, no embedded context needed. Projects kept as Voicer + WeepScope: Voicer (Samsung Top 10, on-device TinyML, audio-to-speech) and WeepScope (1D CNN+GRU on audio signals, co-authored paper) form the strongest audio ML signal pair for a voice AI company.

contacts.research_hook: Featured in gnani.ai's 'People of gnani.ai' YouTube series as Lead NLP Engineer. At Gnani since 2019 (Senior -> Lead). His headline spans multimodal speech + audio LLMs, ASR, TTS, Indic language AI -- the exact intersection of both JDs. He has been through the full Vachana STT/TTS and Inya VoiceOS build over 6+ years.
contacts.subject: People of gnani.ai -- audio LLMs and the full Inya stack
contacts.content:
Hi Avinash,

Caught you in Gnani's 'People of gnani.ai' series. The headline -- multimodal speech and audio LLMs spanning ASR, TTS, and Indic language AI -- reads like you have been at the intersection of every piece of what became Inya VoiceOS. Six years is a long time to watch a voice AI stack mature from conversational bots to a 5-billion-parameter voice-to-voice model.

My work is on the LLM and retrieval side: LangGraph stateful graph handling permission checks, retrieval, tool calls, and generation in sequence; FAISS to Milvus migration that took MRR from 45% to 95% on 50K+ monthly queries; empirical model evaluation from my IIT Delhi research -- 1,000+ configs across CNN-BiLSTM families. Earlier project: 1D CNN+GRU classifying infant cry audio at 86% accuracy, co-authored paper. The audio signal path is one I know from the model side.

If your team is hiring on the ML or NLP side, I would like 15 minutes to hear how the Vachana/Inya pipeline actually fits together. Does next week work?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: People of gnani.ai -- audio LLMs and the full Inya stack
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Avinash,

Following up in case this got buried. Read Gnani's Business Standard interview on Inya VoiceOS after sending the last note -- Ananth's point about specialised SLMs outperforming LLMs for Indic contexts resonated. The evaluation methodology question (how you know a smaller fine-tuned model beats a generic large one on your domain) is exactly the kind of thing I find most interesting on the post-training side. Still curious if there is a fit.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: People of gnani.ai -- audio LLMs and the full Inya stack
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Avinash,

Last note from me. If the timing is not right, no worries -- would still appreciate being on your radar when it comes up.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: Technical Leader - Conversational AI, GenAI, LLM
contacts.research_hook: Came from Quantiphi (known for large-scale ML deployment for Fortune 500) to lead technical delivery at Gnani across Conversational AI, GenAI, and LLM stacks. Previously Accenture. The Quantiphi-to-voice-AI-platform path is specific -- Quantiphi builds ML for enterprises, Gnani deploys voice AI for enterprises; same client type, different layer.
contacts.subject: Quantiphi to Gnani -- enterprise ML, different layer
contacts.content:
Hi Abhilash,

Noticed the Quantiphi -> Gnani move on your profile. Quantiphi builds ML infrastructure for Fortune 500 enterprises; Gnani deploys voice AI for the same enterprise segment. Same clients, one layer deeper in the stack. Makes sense as a next step.

My background is on the LLM and retrieval side: LangGraph stateful graph for multi-tenant agentic RAG handling 50K+ monthly queries, FAISS-to-Milvus migration that doubled MRR (45% to 95%), and a production OCR pipeline for sensitive government documents using on-premise Llama-3.1. Earlier HuggingFace work includes evaluating 1,000+ model configurations for audio classification at IIT Delhi.

If Gnani is building out the GenAI layer and there is an opening, I would be glad to hear what the technical delivery side looks like right now. Open to 15 minutes whenever it fits.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Quantiphi to Gnani -- enterprise ML, different layer
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Abhilash,

Checking back on this. One more detail that might be relevant: the RAG system I built handles 250+ granular permissions per query via 2-stage retrieval -- a Milvus permission index cross-checked against PostgreSQL, then GPT-4o for access control and query rewriting across 10,000+ chunks. Enterprise multi-tenancy at the retrieval layer is the part most production RAG systems skip, and Gnani's enterprise client list suggests it matters here. Would be glad to discuss if the team has room.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Quantiphi to Gnani -- enterprise ML, different layer
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Abhilash,

Last note from me. If now is not the right time, no worries -- happy to reconnect whenever it comes up.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: His LinkedIn headline explicitly lists 'LLM Training' and 'LLM Finetuning' alongside RAG, SLM, and NLP -- he is working across both the post-training and retrieval pipelines, most likely for the Vachana/Inya model stack. Email best-guess: nanda.kumar@gnani.ai (treating Nanda as first name, Kumar as last; name format uncertain for South Indian naming convention where N is a father's initial).
contacts.subject: LLM training and RAG in the same stack
contacts.content:
Hi Nanda,

Your headline is one of the few I have seen that puts LLM Training and Finetuning next to RAG in the same line -- usually those are two different teams. At Gnani, given the Vachana and Inya models, it makes sense that post-training and retrieval would both be in scope for the same engineer.

My work sits at that same intersection: HuggingFace Transformers across 1,000+ model configurations for an IIT Delhi audio classification study, plus production agentic RAG on LangGraph and Milvus serving 50K+ monthly queries. I also built a multi-stage OCR pipeline using DocTR and on-premise Llama-3.1 for structured entity extraction on government documents.

If you are working across both the training and retrieval sides at Gnani and the team has an opening, I would be glad to hear what the stack actually looks like. 15 minutes works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: LLM training and RAG in the same stack
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Nanda,

Following up on the last note. One more thing worth mentioning: a fair amount of my time has gone into systematic benchmarking -- testing which architecture family wins on a given task across 1,000+ configurations with leave-one-subject-out validation at IIT Delhi. That same approach applies on the fine-tuning side, where the question is which alignment method (DPO vs GRPO vs SFT) actually moves the numbers on your domain rather than on a generic benchmark. If that kind of work is what the role involves at Gnani, I would like to know more.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: LLM training and RAG in the same stack
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Nanda,

Last message from me. If the timing is off, happy to reconnect later.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: IIT-Madras researcher building 'Sovereign AI' at Gnani. Published research on textless self-supervised voice conversion using Diffusion-Transformer + conditional flow matching -- directly relevant to the Inya VoiceOS voice-to-voice architecture. One of 12 orgs under the govt IndiaAI Mission. He explicitly uses 'sovereign AI' to describe his work.
contacts.subject: Textless voice conversion and Inya's voice-to-voice stack
contacts.content:
Hi Advait,

Your IIT-M research on textless voice conversion -- Diffusion-Transformer plus conditional flow matching -- caught my attention straight away. That approach to self-supervised voice conversion without phoneme alignment is a meaningful jump from conventional TTS pipelines, and it reads like direct groundwork for how a voice-to-voice foundational model like Inya handles the speech-to-speech path without a text bottleneck.

My work is on the LLM and retrieval side: LangGraph-based agentic RAG serving 50K+ monthly queries, empirical model evaluation (1,000+ configs at IIT Delhi for CNN-BiLSTM audio classification), and a 1D CNN+GRU project classifying infant cry audio with a co-authored paper. I have not worked directly on voice conversion, but the path from IIT research internship to production deployment is similar to mine.

If there is room on the team for someone from the LLM/retrieval side who wants to get closer to the speech model layer, I would be glad to hear what the day-to-day looks like. 15 minutes anytime.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Textless voice conversion and Inya's voice-to-voice stack
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Advait,

Following up. After reading more about the IndiaAI Mission scope -- the 12 selected orgs, the low-resource Indic language mandate -- I got curious about what building for languages like Bodo or Santali actually looks like when standard benchmark data barely exists. The gap between research and production data constraints there seems genuinely hard. That is exactly the kind of problem I want to work on. Still interested if there is any fit.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Textless voice conversion and Inya's voice-to-voice stack
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Advait,

Last note. Happy to reconnect if an opening comes up down the line.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: MTech (By Research) from IIT Mandi -- a program known for production-oriented speech and NLP research. She joined Gnani to deploy that research background into real-time voice AI systems during the Vachana STT/TTS and Inya VoiceOS build. The IIT Mandi research background mirrors Amit's IIT Delhi research internship path.
contacts.subject: IIT Mandi to Gnani -- speech research into production
contacts.content:
Hi Ananya,

The IIT Mandi MTech by Research to Gnani Speech AI path is clear -- that program's reputation is specifically because it is production-oriented rather than purely academic. Joining Gnani during the Vachana STT/TTS and Inya VoiceOS build is good timing for someone coming from that background.

I took a similar path: IIT Delhi research internship on CNN-BiLSTM audio classification (1,000+ model configs evaluated, 12,400 labeled samples from 20 participants), then into production AI engineering. Now I build agentic RAG on LangGraph and Milvus at 50K+ monthly query scale. I want to move closer to the speech and model training layer, which is why both of Gnani's current JDs caught my attention.

If you have 10-15 minutes to talk about what the speech AI work actually involves day-to-day, I would find it useful. Happy to work around your schedule.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: IIT Mandi to Gnani -- speech research into production
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Ananya,

Checking back on the last note. One thing I wanted to add: IIT Mandi has published on low-resource Indic language NLP, which connects directly to what Gnani is building for the IndiaAI Mission (fine-tuning for Bodo, Manipuri, Santali, and others). If your current work at Gnani touches that low-resource fine-tuning pipeline at all, it is exactly the kind of problem I want to work on next. Still interested if there is any room on the team.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: IIT Mandi to Gnani -- speech research into production
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Ananya,

Last message from me. If anything opens up, happy to reconnect.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: MLE focused specifically on Speech ML at Gnani during the Inya VoiceOS build period (active 2025-26). Working on core speech modeling alongside a team that launched India's first voice-to-voice foundational model.
contacts.subject: Speech ML at Gnani during the Inya build
contacts.content:
Hi Anantharaman,

Working on Speech ML at Gnani in 2025-26 means you were building core models while Inya VoiceOS was going from concept to a 5-billion-parameter launch and PM Modi inauguration. That is a compressed and intense build cycle for a first-of-its-kind model in India.

My background is on the LLM and retrieval side, but I have worked with audio signals from the model level: 1D CNN+GRU for infant cry classification (co-authored paper, 86% accuracy on Arduino Nano with 1MB flash), CNN-BiLSTM evaluations at IIT Delhi across 1,000+ configurations on 6-axis IMU gesture data. In production, I build and maintain agentic RAG systems on LangGraph and Milvus at 50K+ monthly query scale. I want to move closer to speech and foundational model work, which is why Gnani is a direct target for me.

If there is a good moment to talk about ML roles on the team, I would take 15 minutes. Just let me know.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Speech ML at Gnani during the Inya build
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Anantharaman,

Following up. One thing that came to mind after the last note: the sub-second latency constraint on a 5B parameter model is a non-trivial inference problem. I have worked through similar latency-under-load issues on the RAG side -- cut response time from 16s to 8s under 500+ concurrent users through query preprocessing and retrieval pipeline changes. The speech inference path has its own set of constraints, but the problem-solving approach is familiar. Curious if the team has an opening for someone from the LLM engineering side.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Speech ML at Gnani during the Inya build
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Anantharaman,

Last note from me. Happy to reconnect if the timing works out later.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Appointed Director of Human Resources at Gnani in May 2026, following the $10M Series B, as one of 8 senior hires. Background: 16+ yrs global HR at Google, Geektrust, and Jiva. He is brand new and actively building the team -- highest-leverage moment to reach him.
contacts.subject: Applied ML Engineer roles -- post-training and RAG (post Series B)
contacts.content:
Hi Samuel,

Reached out after seeing your appointment as Director of HR following the Series B. Two JDs caught my attention: the Applied ML Engineer roles for post-training and for RAG/Agentic AI.

Quick profile: 2+ years in production LLM systems. Built and maintained agentic RAG on LangGraph + Milvus serving 50K+ monthly queries (95% MRR after FAISS migration). Production vLLM deployment at 500+ concurrent users. HuggingFace Transformers and TRL for fine-tuning workflows, including 1,000+ model configurations evaluated empirically at IIT Delhi. Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate certified.

Both JDs match my current stack directly. Happy to send my resume or get on a 15-minute call, whichever is easier.

Amit Gohel
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Applied ML Engineer roles -- post-training and RAG (post Series B)

Hi Samuel,

Following up on the Applied ML Engineer roles. To add one more detail: I have worked across both the RAG/retrieval side (LangGraph stateful graph with 250+ granular permissions per query, Milvus + PostgreSQL 2-stage retrieval) and the model fine-tuning side (HuggingFace TRL, LoRA/QLoRA for production inference optimization). The dual-JD match is genuine. Happy to discuss if there is a screening process in place.

Amit Gohel
